title: silphium perfoliatum subsp. connatum
intro: Silphium perfoliatum subsp. connatum, also known as Tarragon or Mountain Silphium, is a plant species that belongs to the Valerian family (Valerianaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, fever, and menstrual cramps. The plant has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and easing menstrual pain, due to its high concentration of volatile oils and other bioactive compounds. Herbal preparations of Silphium perfoliatum subsp. connatum can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
